title: eriogonum coryphaeum
intro: Eriogonum coryphaeum, also known as sagebrush buckwheat, is a flowering plant native to the western United States, characterized by its clusters of small, white or pink flowers and woolly leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Eriogonum coryphaeum have been shown to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and supporting cardiovascular health. Eriogonum coryphaeum is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and its herbal preparations have been used to treat a range of ailments, from skin irritations to respiratory issues.
